subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The History of OSHA
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The History of OSHA.

hook.text: Before 1970, 14,000 US workers died on the job every year. There were no federal safety laws.

buildup.text: A deadly mine explosion and a chemical plant disaster pushed Congress to act.

discovery.text: OSHA was created in 1970, giving workers the legal right to a safe workplace for the first time.

twist.text: Workplace deaths dropped from 14,000 to under 5,000 per year—despite doubling the workforce.

climax.text: Workers gained the right to refuse dangerous work and request safety inspections.

punchline.text: 14,000 died yearly before safety laws. Laws work when workers die without them.
